rulings.Case Title: WILMINGTON SAVINGS FUND SOCIETY, FSB, DBA VS. 3H RENOVATION SERVICES, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Party
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 6, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF MM CLUB L.L.C.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE PARTY Hearing Required to address legal description of the property. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VERNA BERRY ET AL VS. VANESSA MEANS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 6, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANTS VANESSA MEANS AND BRETT MEANS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES is DENIED. Multiple triable issues of fact exist including but not limited to the scope of the release, which is supplemented by an ambiguous hand-written term; landlord's good faith intent to move-in and landlord's legitimate bona fide reason for failure to move in within 3 months. It is defendant's burden to assert the existence of the release as an affirmative defense and to show that the release covers the claims asserted by Plaintiff. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that it is Plaintiff's burden to seek reformation of the settlement agreement. Furthermore, defendant failed to shift its burden and establish that Plaintiff's causes of action stemming from violation of OMI provisions (last element of the cause of action being failure to timely move-in that have not yet occurred at the time of the settlement), was a known, then-existing claim at the time of the execution of the settlement agreement subject to the release (as opposed to release of all unknown claims). Plaintiff to prepare order in accordance with this ruling and in compliance with CCP 437c(g). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: OKADA BROS, INC. VS. TONY MORENO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 6, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANTS TONY MORENO AND BOBBI ZIMMERMAN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Notice is compliant with CCP 1161(4) and SFRO 37.9(a)(3). Notice is based on alleged nuisance activity, nature of such activity is stated; whether this activity is severe, continuing or recurring and causes substantial interference is an issue of fact not appropriate to be addressed on a demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
